title: Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Abuse Resistance
description: Authenticated users can change identifiers, cross tenant boundaries, automate expensive actions, and exploit support or administrator workflows. Learners cumulatively produce deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose role, attribute, relationship, capability, or policy models from resource, action, subject, context, and review needs.
Enforce authorization at every trusted resource and transition boundary using current server-side state.
Propagate tenant identity through query, cache, file, job, event, log, backup, restore, and support paths with adversarial tests.
